entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The primary crop studied for cytoplasmic male sterility.

entities.name: MAIZE
entities.description: Crop used as a source for mitochondrial DNA probes.

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A condition where male fertility is absent due to cytoplasmic factors.

entities.name: MITOCHONDRIAL DNA
entities.description: The genetic material located in the mitochondria analyzed in the study.

entities.name: RFLP ANALYSIS
entities.description: Restriction Fragment Length Polymorphism analysis used to characterize DNA.

entities.name: SOUTHERN BLOT HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: A method used to detect specific DNA sequences in DNA samples.

entities.name: PSTI
entities.description: A restriction enzyme used to digest mitochondrial DNA.

entities.name: BAMHI
entities.description: A restriction enzyme used to digest mitochondrial DNA.

entities.name: SMAI
entities.description: A restriction enzyme used to digest mitochondrial DNA.

entities.name: XHOI
entities.description: A restriction enzyme used to digest mitochondrial DNA.

entities.name: S-A1 TYPE OF CYTOPLASM
entities.description: A specific classification of cytoplasm in pearl millet based on fertility restoration patterns.

entities.name: FERTILITY RESTORATION
entities.description: The process of restoring fertility to male-sterile lines.

entities.name: COXI
entities.description: A mitochondrial gene used as a probe to identify cytoplasmic groups.

entities.name: ATP9
entities.description: A mitochondrial gene used as a probe to differentiate specific cms lines.

entities.name: COXII
entities.description: A mitochondrial gene used as a probe to detect polymorphism.

entities.name: ATP6
entities.description: A mitochondrial gene used as a probe to identify cytoplasmic groups and subgroups.

entities.name: RRN18-RRN5
entities.description: Genes mapped within pearl millet clones involved in sterility.

entities.name: P. GLAUCUM SUBSP. MONODII
entities.description: A subspecies of pearl millet from which a specific cms line was derived.
